name: Sarah — Agency Creative Director
role: Creative Director or Design Director at a mid-to-large agency (50-500 people), evaluating senior designers or design leads for client-facing product work
goals: Find a designer who can own a client engagement end-to-end without heavy oversight
Confirm the candidate can navigate client stakeholder dynamics, not just internal team collaboration
See evidence of strategic thinking: research, systems, and business context alongside strong visual execution
Hire someone who can present work to clients confidently and defend design decisions under pressure
frustrations: Portfolios that show only in-house product work with no evidence of client-facing skills
Designers who can execute but cannot articulate the 'why' behind decisions to non-designers
Case studies that read as individual contributor work with no sign of leadership or mentorship
No evidence of working across multiple projects simultaneously or managing competing priorities
Portfolios where every project looks the same, suggesting the designer applies one approach to everything
behaviors: Reviews portfolios looking for range: enterprise, consumer, 0-to-1, redesign, research-led
Values designers who have worked with real constraints (NDA, stakeholder overrule, tight timelines)
Looks for evidence of client communication skills in case study framing
Checks if the designer can translate complex problems into clear narratives for non-technical audiences
Often shares portfolio with a client before making the hire, so the portfolio must be client-presentable
Values the portfolio site itself as a craft signal
tools_used: Portfolio URL shared by recruiter or internal referral
Figma (to review design files or component libraries)
Slack or Teams (to share candidate impressions with leadership)
quote: I need someone who can walk into a client kickoff on Monday and run a research debrief by Friday. The portfolio tells me if they can do both.
